data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-05T01:53:39.593Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating this .NET Framework code to .NET 8 and need to ensure all Thread.Abort
usage is addressed. Check this code and provide your Thread.Abort migration guidance.

```csharp
public class SimpleWorker
{
    public void RunBatch(List<WorkItem> items)
    {
        var threads = new List<Thread>();

        foreach (var item in items)
        {
            var t = new Thread(() => Process(item));
            t.Start();
            threads.Add(t);
        }

        foreach (var t in threads)
            t.Join();
    }

    private void Process(WorkItem item)
    {
        // simulate work
        Thread.Sleep(100);
        item.Result = ComputeResult(item.Input);
    }
}
```

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about migrating .NET Framework code that uses Thread.Abort to .NET 8. Let me look at the code they provided - it actually doesn't use Thread.Abort at all. It just uses Thread.Start, Thread.Join, and Thread.Sleep.

Let me invoke the thread-abort-migration skill since it's available and relevant to this request.

Wait, actually, looking more carefully at the code, it doesn't use Thread.Abort, Thread.Interrupt, or ThreadAbortException. The skill description says "DO NOT USE FOR: code that only uses Thread.Join, Thread.Sleep, or Thread.Start without any abort, interrupt, or ThreadAbortException usage."


Since their code doesn't actually use Thread.Abort, I don't need to invoke the skill - I should just let them know their code doesn't have the specific issue they're concerned about.
